name: Irish Tea Bread
recipeInstructions:
Servings: 4
The mixed fruit can include raisins, currants, and any other kind of dried fruit you like; chop any larger pieces down to about raisin size. Place the tea, mixed fruit and sugar in a bowl; cover and leave overnight. Stir in the egg and melted butter. Sieve the flour and bicarbonate of soda together and fold them into the mixture. Line the bottom of a large loaf pan with baking parchment, and brush the whole inside of the pan with melted butter. Pour the mixture into the pan and smooth the top. Bake in a preheated oven (360°F) on the middle shelf for 1 hour and 30 minutes. After removing from the oven, leave in the pan for 2-3 minutes; then turn out onto a wire rack and cool.
